2:T4e8,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How much protein do I need per day?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"The general recommendation is 0.8g per kg of body weight for sedentary adults. For active individuals: 1.2-1.6g/kg for endurance athletes, 1.6-2.2g/kg for strength training and muscle building, and 2.3-3.1g/kg during aggressive fat loss to preserve muscle mass."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can you eat too much protein?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"For healthy individuals, eating more protein than needed is generally safe but unnecessary. Excess protein is either used for energy or converted to fat. Very high protein intake (over 3.5g/kg) may strain kidneys in people with pre-existing kidney disease, but research shows no harm for healthy individuals."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"When should I eat protein for muscle building?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Distribute protein intake evenly across 3-5 meals throughout the day, with 20-40g per meal. Consume protein within 2 hours after workouts to maximize muscle protein synthesis. The 'anabolic window' is less critical than total daily intake, but post-workout protein helps recovery."}}]}7:{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}

border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Fat Loss (Preserve Athletes"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"1.2-1.6g"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"84-112g"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Why Protein Matters"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Protein is the building block of muscle tissue. Every time you work out, you create micro-tears in your muscle fibers. Protein provides the amino acids needed to repair and rebuild these fibers stronger than before — a process called ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"muscle protein synthesis"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Beyond muscle building, protein helps regulate hormones, supports immune function, and increases satiety (feeling full), which makes it easier to maintain a calorie deficit for fat loss."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"How to Calculate Your Protein Needs"}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 1: Determine Your Body Weight"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Use your current body weight in kilograms. If you only know your weight in pounds, divide by 2.2 to get kilograms."}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 2: Choose Your Multiplier Based on Goal"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Sedentary:"}]," 0.8g/kg — minimum to prevent deficiency"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Recreational athlete:"}]," 1.2-1.4g/kg — supports general fitness"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Muscle building:"}]," 1.6-2.2g/kg — maximizes muscle protein synthesis"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fat loss:"}]," 2.0-2.4g/kg — preserves lean mass in a calorie deficit"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Endurance:"}]," 1.2-1.6g/kg — supports recovery from long sessions"]}]]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 3: Do the Math"}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Example:"}]," A 75kg person building muscle would need 75 × 1.8 = ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"135g of protein per day"}],"."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Best Protein Sources"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-4 not-prose my-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/40 rounded-xl p-5","children":[["$","h4",null,{"className":"text-white font-semibold mb-3","children":"Animal Sources"}],["$","ul",null,{"className":"space-y-2 text-slate-400 text-sm","children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Chicken breast: 31g per 100g"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Salmon: 25g per 100g"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Eggs: 6g per large egg"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Greek yogurt: 10g per 100g"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Whey protein: 25g per scoop"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/40 rounded-xl p-5","children":[["$","h4",null,{"className":"text-white font-semibold mb-3","children":"Plant Sources"}],["$","ul",null,{"className":"space-y-2 text-slate-400 text-sm","children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Tofu: 8g per 100g"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Lentils: 9g per 100g (cooked)"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Chickpeas: 9g per 100g"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Quinoa: 4g per 100g (cooked)"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Pea protein: 20g per scoop"}]]}]]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Common Protein Myths"}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Myth 1:"}]," \"You can only absorb 30g of protein per meal.\"",["$","br",null,{}],["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fact:"}]," Your body can absorb more than 30g, but muscle protein synthesis may plateau after 20-40g per meal. Distribute intake across meals for optimal results."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Myth 2:"}]," \"High protein damages kidneys.\"",["$","br",null,{}],["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fact:"}]," Research shows no kidney damage from high protein in healthy individuals. Those with existing kidney disease should consult a doctor."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Myth 3:"}]," \"You need protein immediately after workouts.\"",["$","br",null,{}],["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fact:"}]," The anabolic window is wider than previously thought. Total daily intake matters more than timing."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Related Tools"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/protein-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Protein Calculator"}]," — Get your exact daily target"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/macro-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Macro Calculator"}]," — Calculate protein, carbs, and fat together"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/calorie-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Calorie Calculator"}]," — Find your total daily energy needs"]}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mt-16","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-8","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How much protein do I need per day?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"The general recommendation is 0.8g per kg of body weight for sedentary adults. For active individuals: 1.2-1.6g/kg for endurance athletes, 1.6-2.2g/kg for strength training and muscle building, and 2.3-3.1g/kg during aggressive fat loss to preserve muscle mass."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Can you eat too much protein?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"For healthy individuals, eating more protein than needed is generally safe but unnecessary. Excess protein is either used for energy or converted to fat. Very high protein intake (over 3.5g/kg) may strain kidneys in people with pre-existing kidney disease, but research shows no harm for healthy individuals."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"When should I eat protein for muscle building?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Distribute protein intake evenly across 3-5 meals throughout the day, with 20-40g per meal. Consume protein within 2 hours after workouts to maximize muscle protein synthesis. The 'anabolic window' is less critical than total daily intake, but post-workout protein helps recovery."}]]}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-orange-500 to-orange-600
